questions.question: Explain the three main components of Mobile IP architecture and their functions.
keyPoints: Mobile Node (MN): The device that changes its point of attachment from one network to another
Home Agent (HA): Router in the mobile node's home network that maintains location info and tunnels packets
Foreign Agent (FA): Router in the visited network that provides care-of address and assists in packet delivery

questions.question: Describe the agent discovery process in Mobile IP.
keyPoints: Mobile nodes discover agents through ICMP router advertisements
Agents periodically broadcast advertisements announcing their presence
Mobile nodes can also send solicitation messages to request advertisements
Agents advertise their services and care-of addresses

questions.question: What is the difference between hard handoff and soft handoff in cellular networks?
keyPoints: Hard Handoff: Break-before-make, connection to old base station is terminated before connecting to new one
Soft Handoff: Make-before-break, maintains connections to multiple base stations simultaneously
Soft handoff provides better call quality but requires more complex equipment
Hard handoff is simpler but may cause brief service interruption

questions.question: Explain the registration process in Mobile IP.
keyPoints: Mobile node sends registration request to Foreign Agent with home address and care-of address
Foreign Agent forwards request to Home Agent
Home Agent creates a mobility binding and responds with registration reply
Foreign Agent receives reply and updates its registration table

questions.question: List and explain three major security concerns in wireless networks.
keyPoints: Eavesdropping: Unauthorized interception of wireless signals
Man-in-the-middle attacks: Attacker intercepts communication between two parties
Rogue Access Points: Unauthorized APs that mimic legitimate networks
Solutions include encryption protocols like WEP, WPA, WPA2, WPA3

questions.question: Compare infrastructure mode and ad-hoc mode in wireless networks.
keyPoints: Infrastructure mode uses Access Points (AP) as central hub for all communications
Ad-hoc mode enables direct device-to-device communication without AP
Infrastructure mode suitable for permanent networks, ad-hoc for temporary/special purposes
Ad-hoc networks have limited range and scalability issues

questions.question: Explain the complete operation of Mobile IP including agent discovery, registration, and packet tunneling. Also discuss the triangle routing problem and its solutions.
keyPoints: Agent Discovery: Mobile nodes discover HA and FA through ICMP advertisements/solicitations
Registration: Mobile node registers CoA with HA through FA using registration messages over UDP
Packet Tunneling: HA encapsulates packets and tunnels to CoA; FA decapsulates and delivers to MN
Triangle Routing Problem: Inefficient path through HA when direct route exists
Reverse Tunneling: Packets from MN go through HA before reaching correspondent
Route Optimization: Allows direct routing with binding updates to correspondent

questions.question: Describe the evolution of cellular networks from 1G to 5G. Compare their characteristics in terms of speed, latency, technology, and applications.
keyPoints: 1G: Analog voice only, 2.4 Kbps, FDMA, limited capacity
2G: Digital voice and SMS, 9.6-14.4 Kbps, TDMA/CDMA, GSM
3G: Voice and data, 144 Kbps-2 Mbps, CDMA, video calling
4G: All-IP, 100 Mbps-1 Gbps, LTE, OFDM, HD streaming, mobile broadband
5G: 1-10 Gbps, 1ms latency, massive IoT, AR/VR, smart cities
Key differences: Speed increases exponentially, latency decreases, capacity increases, new use cases emerge

questions.question: Explain the different types of wireless networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN) with examples and their characteristics.
keyPoints: PAN (Personal Area Network): Bluetooth, Zigbee, 1-10 meter range, personal devices
WLAN (Wireless LAN): Wi-Fi (802.11), 100m range, homes/offices
WMAN (Wireless MAN): WiMAX (802.16), city-wide, last-mile connectivity
WWAN (Wireless WAN): Cellular networks (4G/5G), nationwide/global coverage
Characteristics differ in range, speed, frequency band, and applications
